headline: Bernard Arnault's business-first strategy with foreign heads of state, from Trump to Putin
subtitle: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (3/6). The head of LVMH, which generates 70% of its revenue outside Europe, has fostered relationships with the leaders of the markets he targets. Foremost among them is the president of the United States, whom he has courted since the 1980s.

headline: France's environment minister set to resign, after lawmakers reintroduce banned pesticides
French MPs pass law banning social media for under-15s
Zelensky sacks army chief Syrsky after days of protests
subtitle: Volodymyr Zelensky announced he was appointing joint forces commander Mykhailo Drapaty in his place. Questions over Oleksandr Syrsky's future emerged last week, when Zelensky removed highly popular defense minister Mykhailo Fedorov just six months after appointing him.

headline: Sleeping Beauty syndrome: 'For a week, I slept 15 to 20 hours out of every 24'
subtitle: This extremely rare condition, also known as Kleine-Levin syndrome (KLS), mainly affects boys. It is characterized by episodes of massive hypersomnia lasting one or more weeks, often accompanied by cognitive and sometimes behavioral disturbances.

headline: New monkey species identified in Lomami National Park, Democratic Republic of Congo
subtitle: First photographed in 2008, this small colobus monkey with pink-orange lips has now been officially described by an international team of researchers – and may be immediately classified as 'endangered.'

headline: Orléans museum launches 'Wanted' campaign to track down 424 looted artworks
subtitle: Nearly 85 years after Nazi forces looted its collections, the Orléans Museum of Fine Arts is turning to auctioneers, notaries and collectors to help recover the paintings and drawings still missing.

Bernard Arnault, a 'free-marketeer' who whispers in the ear of every president
subtitle: 'Bernard Arnault's Empire' (2/6). LVMH's boss has never hidden his allegiance to France's pro-business, establishment right. He's close to fellow billionaire Vincent Bolloré and, above all, pragmatic. Now he's preparing for the far right's rise to power.

headline: Climate scientists aren't surprised by summer's record heat: 'We've entered a dangerous zone'
subtitle: Record heat, historic drought and sweeping wildfires have marked France's 2026 summer. Yet climate scientists mostly see global warming's expected effects playing out. Only the rapid succession of heatwaves has caught them off guard.

headline: Didier Deschamps bids farewell to French team after spectacular World Cup defeat
subtitle: The coach stepped down after a dramatic 6-4 loss in the World Cup third-place match against England on Saturday. Over his 14 years in the role, he nonetheless managed to restore France to the pinnacle of world football.

headline: China's AI doctrine is a challenge for Europe
